_comment: CRM Action Queue — Rule Engine Configuration. Edit thresholds here without touching Python. CLI flags override at runtime for testing only — they do not write back to this file.

RETENTION_CAMPAIGN._description: High-value customers at material churn risk. Premium retention spend is justified — losing these customers has the biggest GMV impact.

REACTIVATION._description: At-risk customers below median CLV. Worth a lower-cost nudge (voucher, email campaign) — not premium retention spend.

VIP_UPGRADE._description: Champions (top RFM segment) whose predicted CLV is in the 90th percentile. Flag for loyalty programme, early access, or account manager outreach.

MONITOR._description: All other customers. No immediate action required. This is the unconditional catch-all — it MUST be last in evaluation_order and MUST have empty conditions.
MONITOR._note: Do NOT add conditions here. MONITOR catches every customer not matched by the rules above. Adding conditions would create an unmatched-customer gap and crash the pipeline.

_evaluation_note: Rules fire in this order. First match wins. MONITOR is last and unconditional — every customer not matched above lands here. AT_RISK_NURTURE was removed: the crm_action_queue CHECK constraint only allows RETENTION_CAMPAIGN, REACTIVATION, VIP_UPGRADE, MONITOR. At-risk customers (At Risk, Needs Attention, Can't Lose segments) with churn_probability >= 0.6 are captured by REACTIVATION; those below the threshold land in MONITOR. To add AT_RISK_NURTURE back, add it to the CHECK constraint in 05_crm_action_queue.sql first.
_write_mode_note: TRUNCATE_INSERT rebuilds the current-state snapshot every run. History is in mart.action_run_log, not in crm_action_queue itself.

audit._note: Requires 09_action_run_log.sql to have been run. Set write_run_log=false to skip if the table doesn't exist yet.
